records.ety: [F. imputer, L. imputare to bring into the reckoning, charge, impute; pref. im- in + putare to reckon, think. See Putative.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Imputed; p. pr. & vb. n. Imputing.]

defs: To charge; to ascribe; to attribute; to set to the account of; to charge to one as the author, responsible originator, or possessor; -- generally in a bad sense.
quotes: Nor you, ye proud, impute to these the fault, If memory o'er their tomb no trophies raise.
One vice of a darker shade was imputed to him -- envy.

defs: To adjudge as one's own (the sin or righteousness) of another; as, the righteousness of Christ is imputed to us.
quotes: It was imputed to him for righteousness.
They merit Imputed shall absolve them who renounce Their own, both righteous and unrighteous deeds.

defs: To take account of; to consider; to regard.
quotes: If we impute this last humiliation as the cause of his death.
